summary: Exact-match fighter decompilation PR touching four FT files, with 3 bot-reported new matches and additional unmatched-function score improvements. The most substantive review thread resolved an ftcoll.c .sdata2/declaration-order issue by restoring the original .sdata2 block placement, removing extern redeclarations, inlining ordinary float literal uses, and using `if (dmg)` truthiness where MWCC needed variable-first `fcmpu` for matching.

key_files.role: Largest changed file; matched/improved fighter collision functions, introduced inline helpers for item-hit collision handling, adjusted local declaration/order and PAD_STACK shapes, and was the focus of .sdata2/literal review feedback.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftColl_80077970 rewritten with inlineItemA0/inlineItemA1 and 0.5f midpoint literals

key_files.role: Common fighter behavior improvements including item-selection control-flow cleanup, terminal velocity inline access, and expanded island-floor check logic in ftCo_800AB224.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftCo_800A61D8 nested relevant/prio logic replaced with continue filters and itemDist
diff_excerpt: ftCo_800AB224 expands ftCo_800A21FC call into mpIsland_8005AB54/mpIsland_8005AC14 logic
text_corpus issue_comment: ftCo_800AB224 99.99% -> 100.00%; ftCo_800A61D8 and ftCo_800A9904 improved

key_files.role: Samus grapple-related function score improvement via declaration/PAD_STACK changes and localized Item* declaration/access pattern.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: fn_800D9930 declaration order and PAD_STACK(4) changes
review_comments: final verification says fn_800D9930 unchanged at 98.8% on merged head

key_files.role: Kirby special/copy data and matching-shape improvements, including an unsigned costume table type, inline wrapper extraction, joint-reference insertion helper, and PAD_STACK/declaration-order tweaks.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftKb_Init_803CB46C changed from s8 to u8
diff_excerpt: ftKb_SpecialN_800EED50 split into static inline helper plus wrapper
text_corpus issue_comment: ftKb_SpecialN_800EED50, ftKb_SpecialN_800EEC34, ftKb_SpecialN_800EF0E4, and ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C improved

what_changed: PR body claims exact-match decompilation of 4 files and 4 newly matched functions, verified in isolation with `ninja changes_all` and as part of a full ship set with 0 regressions.
Bot report lists 3 new matches in this PR comparison: `ftColl_8007925C`, `ftColl_80077970`, and `ftCo_800AB224`; it also lists 8 additional unmatched-item improvements.
`ftCo_800A61D8` was reshaped from nested conditionals with `relevant`/`prio` locals into early-continue filtering using `ftCo_800A5908`, `inlineD0_it`, kind bounds, priority checks, and `itemDist`.
`ftCo_800AB224` replaced a call to `ftCo_800A21FC(fp)` at the shown site with explicit ground/air, island lookup, position setup, and `mpIsland_8005AC14` comparison logic.
`fn_800D9930` changed local declaration order, PAD_STACK size, and moved the `Item* it` declaration inside a branch before `GET_ITEM(fp->fv.ss.x223C)`.
`ftKb_Init_803CB46C` changed type from `s8` to `u8`, despite initializer entries including `-1`; the PR evidence does not explain the semantic reason.
`ftKb_SpecialN_800EED50` gained a static inline helper called by the externally visible wrapper, and direct table indexing replaced a local `ftKirby_CopyName* copy` temporary.
`ftKb_SpecialN_800EF0E4` extracted a joint-reference insertion loop into `ftKb_SpecialN_800EF0E4_insert_joint_refs` using pointer out-parameters for stack/local shape.
`ftColl_80077970` was decomposed into `inlineItemA0` and `inlineItemA1`, with midpoint computations using `0.5f` literals instead of `ftColl_804D82E8`.
Review iteration in `ftcoll.c` restored the .sdata2 block's original top-of-file ordering, removed extern redeclarations, and inlined ordinary float constant uses as literals.

smart_moves.text: Resolved a data-order/literal matching conflict by preserving original .sdata2 block ordering and inlining ordinary float constants, instead of keeping forward extern redeclarations for constants used inside functions.
evidence_refs: review_comments: ribbanya says replace `ftColl_804D82EC` usages with `1.0f` etc. and do not delete ordering
review_comments: fjooord says `.sdata2` block is back in original spot, extern redeclarations gone, usages inlined as literals

smart_moves.text: Used `if (dmg)` rather than explicit `dmg != 0.0f` after local verification showed MWCC emits variable-first `fcmpu` for the truthiness form, avoiding special extern casts.
evidence_refs: review_comments: fjooord says `if (dmg)` truthiness compiles var-first `fcmpu`, unlike explicit `!= 0.0f`
review_comments: final verification reports ftColl_80077970/ftColl_8007925C still 100% and full ninja build green

smart_moves.text: Extracted repeated or bulky matching-sensitive logic into static inline helpers in `ftcoll.c` and `ftkirby.c`, while keeping public wrapper names where needed.
Converted a deeply nested item-selection ladder in `ftCo_800A61D8` into early `continue` filters, making matching code more readable without losing the reported improvement.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For float constants exposed as .sdata2 symbols, prefer inline literals for ordinary in-function uses when review evidence says data ordering should remain untouched; do not add extern redeclarations merely to force named constant loads unless a specific comparison/codegen case proves it.
evidence_refs: review_comments: ribbanya requested literal inlining and preserving ordering
review_comments: fjooord confirmed extern redeclarations removed and usages inlined as literals

decomp_lessons.lesson: In MWCC float nonzero checks, `if (dmg)` can produce a different compare operand order than `dmg != 0.0f`; in this PR, truthiness matched variable-first `fcmpu` and avoided casted constant-symbol loads.
evidence_refs: review_comments: fjooord final verification of `if (dmg)` var-first `fcmpu`
review_comments: prior comment says explicit `!= 0.0f` regressed three comparisons to about 99.9%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Declaration order, local lifetimes, and PAD_STACK sizes remained matching-sensitive in several FT functions; the PR evidence supports treating these as narrow tactics verified by objdiff/build output, not as general style.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: PAD_STACK changes in fn_800D9930, ftCo_800AB224, ftKb_SpecialN_800EF0E4, ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C
review_comments: PsiLupan notes declaration order was undesirable but removing it was a regression

naming_conventions.name: inlineItemA0 / inlineItemA1
naming_conventions.context: New static inline helper names introduced in `ftcoll.c` for decomposed item-hit collision logic.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: `static inline void inlineItemA0(...)` and `static inline void inlineItemA1(...)`
naming_conventions.notes: Names are functional only at a coarse level and retain generated-like suffixes; no review evidence explains intended semantics.

naming_conventions.name: ftKb_SpecialN_800EED50_inline
naming_conventions.context: Static inline helper paired with public wrapper `ftKb_SpecialN_800EED50`.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: `void ftKb_SpecialN_800EED50` changed to `static inline void ftKb_SpecialN_800EED50_inline`, with wrapper added
naming_conventions.notes: Pattern preserves the original address-style public function name while extracting inlineable body.

naming_conventions.name: ftKb_SpecialN_800EF0E4_insert_joint_refs
naming_conventions.context: Static inline helper name describes the extracted HSD_IDInsertToTable/joint traversal loop.
naming_conventions.notes: More semantic than address-style, but PR evidence only supports the name from the diff, not broader naming policy.

naming_conventions.name: itemDist
naming_conventions.context: `ftCo_800A61D8` now calls `itemDist(fp, ip)` instead of the shown previous `ftCo_800A648C_inline0(fp, ip)` calls.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: `best = itemDist(fp, ip);` and `dist = itemDist(fp, ip);`
naming_conventions.notes: The diff excerpt does not show whether `itemDist` was newly introduced elsewhere or pre-existing.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use boolean float truthiness to influence MWCC `fcmpu` operand order.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The PR discussion reports `if (dmg)` compiled to variable-first `fcmpu`, while `dmg != 0.0f` canonicalized constant-first and caused near-matches instead of exact matches.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Inline ordinary float constants instead of referencing .sdata2 symbols where data ownership/order is not the matching target.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: Review requested replacing `ftColl_804D82EC` usages with literals such as `1.0f`; author later reported .sdata2 ordering restored and extern redeclarations removed.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Preserve or tune declaration order and PAD_STACK only with regression evidence.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: A reviewer explicitly disliked declaration-order dependence but accepted that removing it regressed; diffs show multiple declaration-order and PAD_STACK adjustments.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: fn_800D9930 PAD_STACK(4), ftCo_800AB224 PAD_STACK(0x18), ftKb PAD_STACK changes

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use static inline extraction and pointer out-parameters to shape codegen around large loops or reused collision logic.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The PR introduced `inlineItemA0`, `inlineItemA1`, `ftKb_SpecialN_800EED50_inline`, and `ftKb_SpecialN_800EF0E4_insert_joint_refs`; reported matches/improvements support these as local matching tactics, not general source policy.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: helper extractions in ftcoll.c and ftkirby.c

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use `goto retfalse` to share a return block when matching control-flow shape requires it.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: `ftColl_80077C60` changed a direct `return false` into `goto retfalse` and a shared label at function end, with bot-reported improvement but not full match.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: `return false` changed to `goto retfalse`; `retfalse:` label added

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Declaration order used for matching was disliked, but removing it was known to regress.
review_feedback.resolution: Subsequent comments and final verification indicate the branch retained a matching-safe form; exact final declaration-order details are only partially visible in the truncated diff.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Inline float constant usages as literals, e.g. replace `ftColl_804D82EC` uses with `1.0f`, and do not delete the existing .sdata2 ordering.
review_feedback.resolution: Author reported .sdata2 block restored to original position, extern redeclarations removed, and normal usages inlined as literals.

review_feedback.author: fjooord
review_feedback.feedback: Initial attempt argued some forward declarations were load-bearing to prevent compiler literal pooling; this was revised after review.
review_feedback.resolution: Final comment says `if (dmg)` made extern casts unnecessary and local verification found zero regressions.

follow_up_queries: Find other PRs discussing `if (float)` versus `!= 0.0f` and MWCC `fcmpu` operand order.
Search for `.sdata2` extern redeclarations in `src/melee/ft/ftcoll.c` and whether later PRs removed or standardized them.
Search past PRs for reviewer comments about declaration order being load-bearing or a regression.
Search for other fighter PRs that use static inline wrapper names ending in `_inline` to preserve public function symbols.
Search for `ftColl_804D82E0`, `ftColl_804D82E8`, and `ftColl_804D82EC` handling across later ftcoll cleanup PRs.
Search for `PAD_STACK` adjustments in `ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirby.c` around `ftKb_SpecialN_800EF0E4` and `ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C`.

evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl bot report and review comments
notes: The PR identity, files, author, merge time, and verification claims are directly present in loaded PR metadata/body.
Review feedback about .sdata2 ordering, literal inlining, extern redeclarations, and `if (dmg)` is direct and detailed.
The raw diff is truncated, so file-level summaries are based only on visible hunks plus changed-file metadata and bot report.

The PR body claims 4 newly matched functions, while the loaded bot report excerpt lists 3 new matches and 8 improvements; this record preserves both claims rather than reconciling them beyond available evidence.
No independent build or objdiff was run during intake; validation statements are PR evidence only.

accepted_candidate_records.title: MWCC float truthiness can affect `fcmpu` operand order
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2655, `if (dmg)` was reported to compile variable-first `fcmpu`, while `dmg != 0.0f` canonicalized constant-first and regressed matching in `inlineItemA0`, `inlineItemA1`, and `ftColl_8007925C`. The final branch used truthiness and reported zero regressions.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Do not move .sdata2 ordering or add extern redeclarations when literals suffice
accepted_candidate_records.text: The ftcoll.c review resolved a .sdata2 matching issue by restoring the original .sdata2 block placement, removing extern redeclarations, and inlining ordinary constants such as `1.0f`, `0.5f`, and `±1.0f` as literals.

source_update_candidates.title: Mention float truthiness as a narrow MWCC compare-order tactic
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding a narrow note that, when objdiff shows MWCC float compare operand-order issues, `if (x)` may differ from `x != 0.0f`; keep only with per-function evidence. PR #2655 reports `if (dmg)` produced variable-first `fcmpu` and removed the need for extern constant casts.
source_update_candidates.reason: The behavior is concrete and verified in the PR comments, but should be curated as a narrow tactic rather than promoted automatically.

source_update_candidates.title: Strengthen guidance against extern redeclarations for ordinary float literal loads
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2655 provides a review example where extern redeclarations of .sdata2 float constants were removed and in-function uses were restored to literals while preserving data ordering. This may reinforce existing literal/data-ownership standards.
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded standards already cover literals and data ownership; this PR is a concrete supporting example for curator consideration.

rejection_notes: Do not promote `ftKb_Init_803CB46C` signed-to-unsigned change as a general naming/type lesson; the diff shows it, but no review or body evidence explains the reason.
Do not treat static inline helper extraction as a universal style preference; in this PR it is evidence-backed only as local matching work.
Do not claim the final source has no data diff beyond the author/reviewer statements; intake did not inspect the full final diff or run tools.
Do not infer reviewer intent beyond the explicit comments about declaration order, literal inlining, and .sdata2 ordering.
